name: Madrid Airport Loop
name_original: Entre aeropuertos – circular desde Paracuellos del Jarama – gravel
description: Explore a moderate 18.4-mile gravel cycling loop near Paracuellos del Jarama, offering diverse terrain and unique airport views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Paracuellos De Jarama offer diverse landscapes, from the Jarama River valley to surrounding hills and agricultural fields. The region, known as the "Balcón de Madrid," provides panoramic views, including those of the Madrid-Barajas Airport. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, including paved roads, gravel paths, and some unpaved sections, catering to different preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Paracuellos De Jarama The most popular touring cycling route is Madrid Airport Loop, a 18.4 miles (29.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers unique views of the Madrid-Barajas Airport. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Airplanes Overhead – View of Cobeña loop from Paracuellos de Jarama, a moderate 30.3 miles (48.9 km) path. This route provides extensive views and varied terrain through the local landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Madrid–Barajas Airport – Cerro de las Lámparas loop from Paracuellos de Jarama, a 22.3 miles (35.8 km) trail leading through areas with airport views and local hills, often completed in about 2 hours 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Paracuellos De Jarama is defined by the Jarama River, rolling agricultural fields, and surrounding hills, with routes offering views of the Madrid skyline and airport. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Paracuellos De Jarama are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 230 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Paracuellos De Jarama's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Paracuellos de Jarama?
A: Paracuellos de Jarama offers a wide array of touring cycling routes, with over 200 listed in the broader area. On komoot, you'll find 17 curated touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the difficulty levels for touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The touring cycling routes in Paracuellos de Jarama cater to various skill levels. You'll find 3 easy routes for a relaxed ride, 9 moderate routes offering a balanced challenge, and 5 difficult routes for more experienced cyclists seeking demanding climbs and longer distances.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: You can expect diverse terrain, ranging from paved roads to gravel paths and some unpaved sections. Routes often feature flatter stretches along the Jarama River, rolling agricultural fields, and more demanding climbs into the surrounding hills, providing a varied experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Paracuellos de Jarama?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Paracuellos de Jarama are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962509461/madrid-airport-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madrid Airport Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31499455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Madrid–Barajas Airport – Cerro de las Lámparas loop from Paracuellos de Jarama&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that offer unique views and varied landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Touring cycling routes in Paracuellos de Jarama offer views of the Jarama River valley, agricultural fields, and surrounding hills. You can also enjoy unique views of the Madrid-Barajas Airport on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19200750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Airplanes Overhead – View of Cobeña loop from Paracuellos de Jarama&lt;/a&gt;. The region is known as the 'Balcón de Madrid' for its panoramic vistas, including those from Mirador Picón del Cura.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer excellent viewpoints. The Mirador Picón del Cura provides spectacular panoramic vistas of the Jarama valley, the Madrid skyline, and the airport. Several routes also offer interesting views of the Madrid-Barajas Airport, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31499455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Madrid–Barajas Airport – Cerro de las Lámparas loop from Paracuellos de Jarama&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Paracuellos de Jarama?
A: While cycling is enjoyable year-round, August and October are particularly popular times for touring cyclists in Paracuellos de Jarama. These months often offer favorable weather conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes here?
A: The touring cycling routes in Paracuellos de Jarama are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views of the Jarama River and airport, and the options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or lakes to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region boasts several natural attractions. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelatas Forest&lt;/a&gt;, a preserved forest with numerous trails. For lakes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2859350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Cobeña&lt;/a&gt; and the ecologically valuable Lagunas de Belvis (just 8 km away) are excellent spots for birdwatching and enjoying riparian environments.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the region offers 3 easy-grade routes that could be suitable for families looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature flatter sections and less challenging terrain.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Paracuellos de Jarama?
A: Parking availability varies by starting point. Many routes begin from Paracuellos de Jarama town, where public parking is generally available. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for recommended starting points and potential parking information.

Q: Is it possible to reach the cycling routes by public transport?
A: Paracuellos de Jarama is accessible by public transport from Madrid, primarily via bus services. You can take a bus to the town center, which serves as a convenient starting point for many touring cycling routes in the area. Check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Popularly called "the ball", this is actually a "radome" or, what is the same, a radar covered by a dome (hence its name, in English "radar" + "dome"). It is for this reason that the street on which it is located is called Avenida del Radar.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this gravel cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Paracuellos del Jarama. You can typically find parking within the town itself to begin your ride. Specific trailhead parking isn't explicitly mentioned, but starting points in towns usually offer street parking or small public lots.

faqs.question: Is this gravel cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or on shared paths. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for heat, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for cycling this route. These seasons offer milder temperatures and more comfortable riding conditions. Summer can be very hot, and some sections might become muddy after heavy rain in winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public trails and roads around Paracuellos del Jarama.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this gravel cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, featuring a mix of gravel tracks, cycle paths, greenways, and some paved sections, with less than 20% often being asphalt. You might encounter 'very rolling' sections along the Jarama River, but also unpaved and potentially difficult portions, including sandbanks and rocky areas. A gravel bike or a mountain bike with 29-inch wheels and road tires is recommended.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or viewpoints will I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by several unique points of interest. These include the historical The Serrocales Bunker, the National Institute of Aerospace Technology (INTA), and the distinctive ENAIRE Radar Station, Paracuellos del Jarama. The route also offers unique views of airplanes due to its proximity to the airport, alongside natural landscapes of the Jarama River and countryside.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with official trails like La Cañada Real Galiana, Camino Quintana, and Arco Verde, among others. It also passes through a Trail Junction PRM 40 / PRM 86 / PRM 10, indicating connections to other regional routes.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for all gravel cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty. While shorter, more moderate loops exist in the area, some segments can be challenging, requiring good to very good fitness due to varied surfaces, including unpaved and potentially difficult sections. It's not ideal for absolute beginners but is manageable for cyclists with some gravel experience.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a gravel ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for remote sections, it's wise to pack essentials like a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), plenty of water, snacks, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A fully charged phone with the route downloaded is also highly recommended, as cell service can be spotty in some areas.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Paracuellos del Jarama, you'll find amenities like cafes and shops within the town itself. However, along the trail sections between the airports and along the Jarama River, services are sparse, so plan to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: What's a unique aspect of cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The most unique aspect is the 'Between airports' experience. You get to cycle through peaceful natural settings and rural landscapes, including the Jarama River meadows, while simultaneously enjoying the distinctive sight and sound of airplanes overhead from the nearby airport. It offers a fascinating contrast of nature and aviation close to Madrid.

waypoints.name: ENAIRE Radar Station, Paracuellos del Jarama
